Class ListConversions

java.lang.Object
dev.dejvokep.boostedyaml.utils.conversion.ListConversions
public class ListConversions extends Object
Utility class used to convert raw lists to list of target types.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    ListConversions()
     

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static Optional<List<BigInteger>>
    toBigIntList(List<?> value)
    Constructs a list of big integers from the given list of unknown type.
    static Optional<List<Byte>>
    toByteList(List<?> value)
    Constructs a list of bytes from the given list of unknown type.
    static Optional<List<Double>>
    toDoubleList(List<?> value)
    Constructs a list of doubles from the given list of unknown type.
    static Optional<List<Float>>
    toFloatList(List<?> value)
    Constructs a list of floats from the given list of unknown type.
    static Optional<List<Integer>>
    toIntList(List<?> value)
    Constructs a list of integers from the given list of unknown type.
    static Optional<List<Long>>
    toLongList(List<?> value)
    Constructs a list of longs from the given list of unknown type.
    static Optional<List<Map<?,?>>>
    toMapList(List<?> value)
    Constructs a list of maps from the given list of unknown type.
    static Optional<List<Short>>
    toShortList(List<?> value)
    Constructs a list of shorts from the given list of unknown type.
    static Optional<List<String>>
    toStringList(List<?> value)
    Constructs a list of strings from the given list of unknown type.

  • Constructor Details

    • ListConversions

      public ListConversions()

  • Method Details

    • toStringList

      @NotNull public static Optional<List<String>> toStringList(@Nullable List<?> value)
      Constructs a list of strings from the given list of unknown type. The returned optional is never empty, unless the given value is null.

      The individual elements of the list are processed each one by one. If there is an element incompatible, it is skipped and will not appear in the returned list. Please learn more about compatible types at the main content method Section.getOptionalString(Route).

      Parameters:
      value - the list to construct
      Returns:
      list of strings

    • toIntList

      @NotNull public static Optional<List<Integer>> toIntList(@Nullable List<?> value)
      Constructs a list of integers from the given list of unknown type. The returned optional is never empty, unless the given value is null.

      The individual elements of the list are processed each one by one. If there is an element incompatible, it is skipped and will not appear in the returned list. Please learn more about compatible types at the main content method Section.getOptionalInt(Route).

      Parameters:
      value - the list to construct
      Returns:
      list of integers

    • toBigIntList

      @NotNull public static Optional<List<BigInteger>> toBigIntList(@Nullable List<?> value)
      Constructs a list of big integers from the given list of unknown type. The returned optional is never empty, unless the given value is null.

      The individual elements of the list are processed each one by one. If there is an element incompatible, it is skipped and will not appear in the returned list. Please learn more about compatible types at the main content method Section.getOptionalBigInt(Route).

      Parameters:
      value - the list to construct
      Returns:
      list of big integers

    • toByteList

      @NotNull public static Optional<List<Byte>> toByteList(@Nullable List<?> value)
      Constructs a list of bytes from the given list of unknown type. The returned optional is never empty, unless the given value is null.

      The individual elements of the list are processed each one by one. If there is an element incompatible, it is skipped and will not appear in the returned list. Please learn more about compatible types at the main content method Section.getOptionalByte(Route).

      Parameters:
      value - the list to construct
      Returns:
      list of bytes

    • toLongList

      @NotNull public static Optional<List<Long>> toLongList(@Nullable List<?> value)
      Constructs a list of longs from the given list of unknown type. The returned optional is never empty, unless the given value is null.

      The individual elements of the list are processed each one by one. If there is an element incompatible, it is skipped and will not appear in the returned list. Please learn more about compatible types at the main content method Section.getOptionalLong(Route).

      Parameters:
      value - the list to construct
      Returns:
      list of longs

    • toDoubleList

      @NotNull public static Optional<List<Double>> toDoubleList(@Nullable List<?> value)
      Constructs a list of doubles from the given list of unknown type. The returned optional is never empty, unless the given value is null.

      The individual elements of the list are processed each one by one. If there is an element incompatible, it is skipped and will not appear in the returned list. Please learn more about compatible types at the main content method Section.getOptionalDouble(Route).

      Parameters:
      value - the list to construct
      Returns:
      list of doubles

    • toFloatList

      @NotNull public static Optional<List<Float>> toFloatList(@Nullable List<?> value)
      Constructs a list of floats from the given list of unknown type. The returned optional is never empty, unless the given value is null.

      The individual elements of the list are processed each one by one. If there is an element incompatible, it is skipped and will not appear in the returned list. Please learn more about compatible types at the main content method Section.getOptionalFloat(Route).

      Parameters:
      value - the list to construct
      Returns:
      list of floats

    • toShortList

      @NotNull public static Optional<List<Short>> toShortList(@Nullable List<?> value)
      Constructs a list of shorts from the given list of unknown type. The returned optional is never empty, unless the given value is null.

      The individual elements of the list are processed each one by one. If there is an element incompatible, it is skipped and will not appear in the returned list. Please learn more about compatible types at the main content method Section.getOptionalShort(Route).

      Parameters:
      value - the list to construct
      Returns:
      list of shorts

    • toMapList

      @NotNull public static Optional<List<Map<?,?>>> toMapList(@Nullable List<?> value)
      Constructs a list of maps from the given list of unknown type. The returned optional is never empty, unless the given value is null.

      The individual elements of the list are processed each one by one. If there is an non-map element, it is skipped and will not appear in the returned list.

      Parameters:
      value - the list to construct
      Returns:
      list of maps
